title: Feedback cross-source spot check on committed video fixture

metricComparisons.description: the freeze-smear / feedback-PM mapping should retain more audible energy than the dry baseline on the exported mix

expectedVideo: video fixture should keep advancing while feedback introduces visible persistence and trail buildup
high feedback should not pin the frame or crush motion into a static smear
expectedAudio: fixture audio should gain a smeared freeze tail without clipping or losing channel integrity
wet feedback should remain intelligible at a strong setting on non-procedural material
manualChecks: Does the same feedback law stay usable on decoded media, not just procedural osc?
Does high feedback preserve motion detail and avoid obvious runaway build-up?
Does the decoded-video fixture show clear persistence/trailing by eye even though temporalDiff is no longer stable enough to hard-gate that visual side?
